items.title: Energy-Hungry Asia Is Already Drawing Lessons From Iran Crisis
items.summary: There is still no lasting peace deal in the Persian Gulf, but energy-hungry Asia is already drawing energy lessons from four months of war: it needs bigger buffers, a greater diversity of fossil-fuel suppliers, and a better mix of power sources overall.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Is super-ageing Singapore headed for population decline?
items.summary:
Five years into her marriage to a finance executive, Clare* is still doing the sums on leaving behind a life unencumbered by the high price of parenthood.
At 31, the Singaporean doctor instead finds herself wondering if she will feel different about their “dink” (dual income, no kids) lifestyle in another three years.
“The thought of having kids also feels more like an obligation just to complete

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.
